body.title: Unified memory node rollout: migrate remaining tables after pilot validates
body.result: tags+triggers+GIN on 4 tables + compiled-inference registry (node_registry.py, 6 types, 12 tests) + _cross_type_fts in recall.py wired into escalation path. Commit 1ef885f6.
body.description:
After T-unified-memory-node-pilot ships and at least 12 examples of the unified node format are live and searchable, roll out the ID/METADATA/TRIGGERS/TAGS/DATA_PAYLOAD outer shell to remaining tables: tickets (clan.memories procedural), palace nodes (adc.palace), channel messages (infra.channel_messages), reading items, eval history, etc.

Each table migration produces: a schema change, a prebuilt view so existing consumers are unchanged, and any compiled-inference query patterns specific to that type (e.g. 'tickets due today' as a materialized view rather than an ad-hoc query).

The compiled inference vision: pre-built constrained queries per node type replace general ad-hoc searches. The system knows the access patterns at design time and compiles them in. General queries remain possible but are not the default path.

**Affected files:** multiple schema migrations + view definitions; one file per table type
**Design rules:** no SQLite; each migration is independently deployable; views maintain backward compat
**Scope boundary:** IN — all tables not covered by T-unified-memory-node-pilot; prebuilt views per type; compiled-inference query registry; OUT — changing consumer code beyond view wiring
**Completion criteria:** all major tables use unified outer shell; a compiled-inference query registry exists mapping node type → canonical prebuilt query; search across type boundaries (memory.all contains foo) works correctly
